creator: yetoohappy@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Warner Losh from comment #2)
I tried usb_quirk with the 15.0-CURRENT image live usb I was using before. On a fresh boot, I tried kldload usb_quirk but it said the module was already loaded despite me not seeing it in kldstat or kldstat -v. I executed usbconfig ugen0.3 add_quirk UQ_CFG_INDEX_1 (with ugen0.3 being the usb to ethernet device) then set ue0 to 192.168.1.1/24 so pings from the Windows laptop succeeded then put the laptop to sleep and woke it back up and the pings failed. On a new boot of live usb I did the same, but instead of putting computer to sleep I physically disconnected the cable and ping still fails. usbconfig -d X.Y set_config 1 resolved the issue like last time (set_config 1 disconnects the interface in such a way to remove the ip I assigned it so I had to reassign 192.168.1.1/24 to confirm pings from Windows laptop would succeed).
